summary: Home-Run Contest stage decomp PR centered on src/melee/gr/grhomerun.c. It implemented several previously stubbed or partially matched grhomerun functions, added local state typing and helper declarations, updated grhomerun.h prototypes/includes, and made small include/style cleanups in neighboring fighter, game-mode, library, menu, and stage files. The bot report records 4 new matches and 5 unmatched-item improvements for main/melee/gr/grhomerun.

key_files.role: Primary decomp target; adds implementations for grHomeRun_8021CB20, grHomeRun_8021D680, grHomeRun_8021E500, fn_8021E994 and matching-oriented edits to nearby functions.
Header/prototype surface updated for grhomerun functions and mp forward declarations.
Include ordering/path cleanup in Popo SpecialLw source.
Formatting cleanup for GXColor compound literals.
Whitespace-only cleanup near existing PAD_STACK use.
Small one-line stage-related change; exact hunk not present in loaded excerpt.
Small type-header addition; exact hunk not present in loaded excerpt.
Small one-line deletion; exact hunk not present in loaded excerpt.
Small one-line deletion; exact hunk not present in loaded excerpt.
Small one-line deletion; exact hunk not present in loaded excerpt.

what_changed: Implemented several grhomerun functions that were previously marked with decomp placeholders, including grHomeRun_8021CB20, grHomeRun_8021D680, grHomeRun_8021E500, and fn_8021E994.
Added grHomeRun_MainGroundVars as a local struct over gp->gv.homerun, with fields for object arrays, text/camera-related objects, rear background GObjs, and an xE8 bitfield.
Added a no-inline wrapper grHomeRun_8021C82C_noinline around grHomeRun_8021C82C using scoped pragmas.
Replaced raw or unknown header declarations for at least grHomeRun_8021E500 and fn_8021E994 with typed declarations in grhomerun.h, and added mp/forward.h.
Changed grHomeRun_8021EDD4 from taking an int parameter to void and updated the call in grHomeRun_8021C754.
Adjusted grHomeRun_8021EA30 arithmetic into temporaries and simplified the US-language conversion expression; bot report says this function reached 100%.
Changed grHomeRun_8021EC58 from u32 score to s32 arg0, used a static local format string symbol grHr_804D49D0 for "%d", and used external grHr_804DBC* data symbols for color/float inputs.
Made minor include-order, formatting, and whitespace changes outside grhomerun.c.

smart_moves.text: The main stage work uses a recovered local struct for Home-Run ground vars instead of expressing every access as raw offsets.
Function bodies and header prototypes were advanced together for grhomerun symbols, reducing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS exposure at the interface.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt:fn_8021E994(Ground*, s32, CollData*, s32, mpLib_GroundEnum, f32)

smart_moves.text: Repeated background/object setup is represented with loops over gobjs/jobjs arrays rather than pasted repeated blocks.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt:for loops shifting vars->gobjs in grHomeRun_8021D680

smart_moves.text: The PR had bot-reported matching evidence for the touched grhomerun unit: 4 new matches and 5 unmatched-item improvements.

decomp_lessons.lesson: For stage ground-state recovery, a temporary local struct over a known GroundVars union arm can make large functions reviewable while preserving address-style field names for unknown semantics.
When repeated spawned-object handling is indexed by stage segment or camera range, natural loops can be compatible with matching progress.
Header signature cleanup can accompany body recovery when the diff proves argument and return types through callsites and implementation.

naming_conventions.name: grHomeRun_MainGroundVars
naming_conventions.convention: Semantic temporary struct name for Home-Run main ground state, while uncertain fields remain address-style such as xCC, xD0, xD4, and xE8_flags.

naming_conventions.name: rear_gobj, rear2_gobj, rear3_gobj, rear4_gobj
naming_conventions.convention: Use short role-based local/field names when the repeated background object role is apparent in source flow.

naming_conventions.name: grHomeRun_8021C82C_noinline
naming_conventions.convention: Wrapper name preserves the address-style target name and appends the matching tactic role rather than inventing new semantics.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Scoped pragma dont_inline wrapper around grHomeRun_8021C82C to call through grHomeRun_8021C82C_noinline.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk_note: Loaded evidence shows the tactic and bot-reported score improvements, but not an explicit reviewer or author explanation for why noinline was required.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: PAD_STACK(8) in grHomeRun_8021CB20 and existing PAD_STACK(16) context in gmresultplayer.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk_note: PAD_STACK use is a narrow matching tactic; the loaded bot report provides progress but no adjacent-function regression discussion.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use of temporary arithmetic variables in grHomeRun_8021EA30 and grHomeRun_8021EC58 to influence expression/codegen shape.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk_note: Supported as observed source shape only; not enough evidence to generalize beyond this PR.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Introduced static char grHr_804D49D0[] = "%d" and extern grHr_804DBC* data symbols for text rendering/data matching.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt:extern GXColor grHr_804DBC74 and f32 grHr_804DBC78/7C/80/84
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk_note: This is data/literal-sensitive work; loaded evidence does not include source ownership rationale for the string/data symbols.

follow_up_queries: Search later grhomerun PRs for removal or retention of grHomeRun_8021C82C_noinline and #pragma dont_inline.
Search for subsequent cleanup or relocation of grHr_804D49D0 and grHr_804DBC* data symbols.
Search current grhomerun.h for whether fn_8021E994 and grHomeRun_8021E500 signatures remained stable.
Search for later PRs that finish grHomeRun_8021CB20, grHomeRun_8021D680, or grHomeRun_8021E500 from partial percentages.
Search for grHomeRun_MainGroundVars or gv.homerun field names to see if xCC/xD0/xD4/xE8_flags were later semantically renamed.

notes: Strong evidence for PR identity, merge state, changed-file list, main diff shape, and bot-reported matching progress.
PR body is empty and there are no human review comments, so author intent and reviewer rationale cannot be inferred.
The raw diff loaded into context is truncated; some small changed-file hunks are known only from changed-file metadata, not exact code excerpts.
Bot report provides score/match evidence but not detailed objdiff, build logs, adjacent regression checks, or rationale for matching tactics.
Data/literal edits in grhomerun.c are visible, but loaded evidence does not prove section ownership rationale beyond reported .sdata/.sdata2 improvements.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Local struct overlay for Home-Run ground vars improved grhomerun decomp readability
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2443 introduced grHomeRun_MainGroundVars over gp->gv.homerun and used it through grHomeRun_8021CB20, grHomeRun_8021D680, and fn_8021E994 while keeping uncertain fields address-style. This is useful evidence for typed-fields-over-pointer-math in stage ground code, but only as a PR-specific example.

accepted_candidate_records.title: PR 2443 grhomerun matching progress
accepted_candidate_records.text: The bot report says fn_8021E994, grHomeRun_8021C754, grHomeRun_8021EA30, and grHomeRun_8021EDD4 became 100% matched; grHomeRun_8021CB20, grHomeRun_8021D680, grHomeRun_8021E500, .sdata2, and .sdata improved but remained not fully matched in the report.

source_update_candidates.title: grhomerun.c owns Home-Run Contest stage decomp work in PR 2443
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2443's main changes were in src/melee/gr/grhomerun.c and the bot report identifies the affected unit as main/melee/gr/grhomerun, with new matches and improvements for grHomeRun_8021CB20, grHomeRun_8021D680, grHomeRun_8021E500, fn_8021E994, grHomeRun_8021C754, grHomeRun_8021EA30, and grHomeRun_8021EDD4.
source_update_candidates.reason: Useful path ownership/search fact, but should be curated against current source before promotion.

source_update_candidates.title: Require rationale for noinline wrappers used as matching aids
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2443 shows a scoped #pragma dont_inline wrapper grHomeRun_8021C82C_noinline used in matching-oriented grhomerun code. Existing standards already caution that pragmas need evidence; this PR may be a concrete example if later curation can verify the tactic was necessary and did not harm neighbors.
source_update_candidates.reason: Candidate example only; loaded evidence lacks explicit author/reviewer rationale and adjacent regression proof.

source_update_candidates.title: Review string literal promotion in matching PRs against data ownership evidence
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2443 introduced static char grHr_804D49D0[] = "%d" and reported .sdata/.sdata2 improvements. This should be reviewed as an example for the existing literal/data ownership standards, not accepted as a general pattern.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potentially relevant to standards, but current loaded PR evidence does not prove the data ownership rationale.

rejection_notes: Do not promote the noinline wrapper as generally acceptable style; the loaded slice only shows it existed and that the bot reported progress.
Do not infer that all grHr_804DBC* externs or grHr_804D49D0 are correct current data ownership facts from this PR alone.
Do not treat the minor formatting changes in gm_18A5.c, gmclassic.c, and gmresultplayer.c as substantive decomp lessons.
Do not claim full matches for grHomeRun_8021CB20, grHomeRun_8021D680, or grHomeRun_8021E500; the bot report lists them as improvements in unmatched items, not 100% matches.
